observations.text: Synthetic 3/2 neoclassical tearing mode: a rotating island that grows then saturates without locking. For UI and contract validation only.

hypothesis: A 3/2 NTM saturates while continuing to rotate.
limitations: This record uses SYNTHETIC educational data only.
This is not FAIR MAST data and is not from any real device.
This record is for read-only analysis and decision support.
This record has no command/control path or hazardous operating procedure.
This record is not a standalone authority for safety-critical operation or reactor design decisions.

shot.notes: Synthetic 3/2 neoclassical tearing mode: a rotating island that grows then saturates without locking. Not real measurements.

observationModels.label: Analytic 3/2 neoclassical tearing mode
assumptions: Metastable island sustained by bootstrap-current drive.
limitations: Analytic model, not a facility simulation.
Island width uses an educational sqrt-amplitude scaling.

inferences.label: Phase-fit mode estimate for the Mirnov toroidal array
assumptions: Single dominant rotating mode within the analysis window.
limitations: Toroidal mode number resolved only up to |n| <= 4 (Nyquist for 8 probes).
Island width is an educational sqrt-amplitude estimate, not a calibrated reconstruction.

claims.statement: A 3/2 neoclassical tearing mode grows to a saturated, steadily rotating island (no locking) that degrades confinement.

evidence.rationale: Phase fit recovers n=2 (m=3) with rotation that stays ~4 kHz, i.e. growth without locking.
assumptions: Single dominant rotating mode within the analysis window.
limitations: Mediated evidence link; not direct phenomenon observation.

evidence.rationale: Fluctuation amplitude saturates, consistent with a metastable NTM island reaching steady width.
assumptions: Single dominant rotating mode within the analysis window.
limitations: Mediated evidence link; not direct phenomenon observation.
